# SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
#
# lunar_differential_pnt external-oracle reference fixture.
#
# ORACLE: RTKLIB relative/DGPS positioning kernel, compiled from C source.
#   Repo    : github.com/tomojitakasu/RTKLIB
#   Version : library string "2.4.2", git tag v2.4.2-p13, commit 71db0ff (2018-01-30);
#             the 2.4.3 line carries this same rtkcmn.c lsq()/matinv() kernel.
#   Author  : T. Takasu.   License: BSD 2-Clause (+ non-exclusivity clauses).
#   Files   : src/rtkcmn.c -> dot(), norm(), lsq() [x = (A A^T)^-1 A y normal-equations
#             WLS], matmul()/matinv()/ludcmp()/lubksb() (pure-C path, built with -ULAPACK
#             so no BLAS/LAPACK is linked). lsq() is the kernel pntpos.c::estpos() uses
#             for the standalone/relative position solve.
#
# QUANTITIES (computed by RTKLIB on kshana's exact injected geometry):
#   (1) per-satellite single-difference corrected pseudorange residual
#         sd_i = -e_i . (u_user - u_ref)  [m]   (common clock cancels), and
#   (2) the 3-D user position-error magnitude from a 4-param (x,y,z,clk) WLS solve.
#
# MATCHED TO: kshana lunar_dpnt::corrected_user_range_errors (after
#   differential_corrections) for the per-sat residuals, and
#   user_position_error_m(.., apply_corrections=true) for the position error.
#   Tolerance gate: residuals < 1e-9 m, position error < 1e-6 m (relative+abs).
#
# HONEST SCOPE / INDEPENDENCE: the geometry (Keplerian sat positions,
#   selenographic->MCMF, injected orbit+clock errors) is kshana's own and is fed to the
#   oracle as given numeric inputs; it is NOT re-derived in C. What is independent is the
#   COMPUTATION on it -- RTKLIB's compiled-C dot/norm for the LOS-difference residual and
#   RTKLIB's lsq()/matinv() LU inverse for the position solve, vs kshana's hand-rolled
#   invert4. Both implement the SAME first-order LOS-difference + (G^T G)^-1 G^T WLS
#   algebra, so this is an internal-consistency / shared-algorithm cross-check between two
#   independent code bases and two independent 4x4 inverters -- it catches algebra /
#   indexing / conditioning bugs, NOT modelling-assumption errors.
#
# GRID: baselines {0,1,10,50,100,250,500} km x seeds {42,7} = 14 cases, 8 sats each.
#
# REPRODUCE: see generate_lunar_differential_pnt_reference.sh + oracle.c in this dir.
#
# FORMAT (per case block):
#   CASE   <seed> <baseline_km> <n_sats>
#   ORACLE <seed> <baseline_km> <n_sats> <poserr_corrected_m> <max_abs_sd_m> <sd_0 .. sd_{n-1}>
#   REF    <rx> <ry> <rz>                      (reference-station MCMF, m)
#   USER   <ux> <uy> <uz>                      (user MCMF, m)
#   SAT    <sx> <sy> <sz> OE <ex> <ey> <ez> CE <c>   (n lines: sat MCMF m; orbit-err vec m; clock-err m)
#
CASE 42 0 8
ORACLE 42 0 8 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00 0.00000000000000000e+00
REF 3.03218109441365232e4 0.00000000000000000e0 -1.73713538556471420e6
